objective: Assessed the plan for migrating legacy .grove/explore.json to .grove/config.json on GroveConfig::load.
key_changes: Verified plan against actual source: GroveConfig, Mode::McpLlm, Steering::from_name, Provider::from_name, ExploreConfig::config_path, and GroveConfig::save all exist and are usable as described.
Three-branch load cascade (config.json -> explore.json migration -> grove init bail) is correct; ExploreConfig already in scope, no new import needed.
AC1/AC2 satisfied: from_name calls validate + remap old mode->steering; migration preserves provider, base_url, model, allowed_tools, tap, trace_retain with no data loss.
AC3 one-time warning is structurally guaranteed: config.json presence routes subsequent loads to branch 1; test 5b confirms idempotency.
AC-to-test mapping complete (5a-5d) plus existing missing_file_actionable_error guards AC4.
ADVISORY: default_trace_retain is private in explore/config.rs (only DEFAULT_TRACE_RETAIN const is pub) - serde default ref won't compile from config.rs; use a local fn or the pub const.
ADVISORY: test 5d stderr capture is fragile (plan admits weak CI-output fallback); recommend a testable warning const + side-effect assertion instead.
ADVISORY: malformed explore.json yields Err rather than the grove init steer - acceptable but document the branch semantics.
findings: Plan is feasible and complete; all 6 acceptance criteria map to concrete steps and tests. Approved with three non-blocking advisory notes.
